description: An inner subscribe nested inside an outer one loses cancellation, so a stale response overwrites a newer one.
body: Failure mode: code subscribes to a source stream and, inside that callback, subscribes to a dependent request, so rapid source emissions fire overlapping inner requests with no cancellation and a slow older response lands after a newer one. Detection signals: search/typeahead shows results for a previous query; out-of-order responses; a .subscribe inside another .subscribe instead of a flattening operator. Do not nest subscribes for dependent async work. Safer fallback: pipe the source through switchMap (to cancel stale inner streams), concatMap (to preserve order), or exhaustMap (to ignore while busy), and prove cancellation with a marble test.
